title: marlea tomentosa
intro: Marlea tomentosa, also known as Marama or woolly marlea, is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Marlea tomentosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Marlea tomentosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
